title: UK Says Falkland Islands Belong to Britain
summary: The Falkland Islands are a group of islands in the Atlantic Ocean. They are part of the United Kingdom (UK), but Argentina also says they belong to them. In 1982, there was a war between the UK and Argentina over the islands. The UK won, and the people who live there voted to stay with the UK. Recently, a news report said the United States (US) might change its mind about who owns the islands. The UK government said the islands belong to Britain and that the people who live there have the right to choose. The US has not said anything official yet. The UK says its position is clear and will not change.

keywords.explanation: who has the power to rule a place
a group of islands in the Atlantic Ocean, controlled by the UK
the right of people to decide their own government

options: The US might change its position on the islands

questions.correct_answer: The US might change its position on the islands

questions.question: What did the UK government say about the report?
options: The UK will give up the islands
The UK's position is clear and will not change

questions.question: What is self-determination?
options: The right of people to choose their own government
questions.correct_answer: The right of people to choose their own government

background_read: The Falkland Islands are located in the South Atlantic Ocean, about 300 miles east of Argentina. The islands have been under British control since 1833, except for a brief period during the Falklands War in 1982. The population is about 3,000 people, mostly of British descent. In a 2013 referendum, 99.8% of voters chose to remain a British Overseas Territory. Argentina continues to claim sovereignty, calling the islands the Malvinas.
Article_Structure: WHO: The UK government, the US government, and the people of the Falkland Islands.
WHAT: The UK says the Falkland Islands belong to Britain after a report that the US might change its position.
WHERE: The Falkland Islands are in the South Atlantic Ocean. The news is reported from London and Washington.
WHY: The UK wants to make clear that the islands are British because the US might be thinking differently.

perspectives.description: The UK believes the islands belong to them and the islanders have the right to choose.
The US might be considering a change, but has not made an official statement.
Argentina claims the islands and wants them back, but the UK disagrees.
